_source: MEROPS database (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/merops/); Gomi et al. 1987 (ALP); Tominaga & Tsujisaka 1976 (NPr); Koaze et al. 1964 (acid protease); salt-inhibition data from Tominaga et al. 1982 and Ikeda et al. 1975
_notes: Specificity encoded as P1 (residue before cut) and P1_prime (residue after cut). Lists are the preferred amino acids at each position. Empty list means 'any'. Endo-protease = cleaves within chain; exo = N/C-terminal only.

ALP._ph_note: Conservative: ALP is outside its active pH range at shio-koji pH 4.5-5.0 (active range 6.0-12.0), so true pH factor should be ~0 (inactive). Set to 1.0 to be conservative — real risk is lower than computed.
ALP.notes: Broad specificity; major secreted protease in A. oryzae koji. Prefers hydrophobic/aromatic at P1 but tolerates many residues. Substantially inhibited by NaCl.

salt_inhibition.source: Tominaga et al. 1982; Ikeda et al. 1975 — A. oryzae alkaline subtilisin inhibited ~80-90% at 15-20% NaCl

NPr._ph_note: Conservative: NPr active range is 5.0-9.0; shio-koji pH 4.5-5.0 is at the edge. True pH factor is likely 0.3-0.5. Set to 1.0 to be conservative.
NPr.notes: Thermolysin-like specificity: cleaves before (N-terminal to) hydrophobic bulky residues. Requires zinc ion. Moderately salt-sensitive.

salt_inhibition.source: Tominaga & Tsujisaka 1976 — NPr retains more activity than ALP under salt conditions

acid_protease._ph_note: ~30% of pH-optimal activity at shio-koji pH 4.5-5.0 (near upper edge of active range 2.0-5.5). Source: Koaze et al. 1964 activity-vs-pH curve.
acid_protease.notes: Pepsin-like: prefers hydrophobic/aromatic at both P1 and P1'. Active only under acidic conditions. Shio-koji pH ~4.5-5.0 is near the upper edge of its active range — partially active. Relatively salt-tolerant compared to ALP/NPr.

salt_inhibition.source: Koaze et al. 1964; aspartic proteases generally more salt-tolerant than serine/metalloproteases

shio_koji_conditions.notes: Standard shio-koji: ~15-20% NaCl, RT ferment 7-14 days. pH driven by kojic acid and organic acids produced during initial koji growth.
